entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The agricultural practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same piece of land

entities.name: TRADITIONAL FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Conventional agricultural systems based on long-standing local practices

entities.name: LARGE FARMS
entities.description: Agricultural holdings characterized by extensive land area

entities.name: IRRIGATED FARMS
entities.description: Agricultural holdings that use artificial irrigation systems for water supply

entities.name: SMALL FARMS
entities.description: Agricultural holdings characterized by limited land area

entities.name: RAIN-FED FARMS
entities.description: Agricultural holdings that depend solely on precipitation for water

entities.name: INTERCROPPING TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technical innovations and methodologies designed to optimize intercropping systems

entities.name: POORLY-ENDOWED FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers with limited access to financial, land, or technical resources

entities.name: WELL-ENDOWED FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers with substantial access to financial, land, or technical resources

entities.name: SPATIAL ARRANGEMENTS
entities.description: The physical organization and layout of different crops within an intercropping system

entities.name: CROP COMBINATIONS
entities.description: The specific selection and pairing of different crop species grown together

entities.name: FARMER'S MULTIPLE OBJECTIVES
entities.description: The diverse and simultaneous goals, such as risk reduction and yield maximization, that farmers seek to achieve
